mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals increasing labor market tightness, which raises expectations of future wage inflation and prompts the bond market to demand higher nominal yields to compensate for inflation risk, pushing the 10-year treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward yield changes are negative in >60% of instances when UNRATE declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds the threshold level of 246, the market interprets this as a sign of persistent inflation, increasing expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which drives up 10-year treasury yields over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After trigger activation, 60-day forward yields decline in more than half of occurrences, or the signal's information coefficient is not significantly positive at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) reflect broad-based economic strength and rising capacity utilization, leading the bond market to price in higher real growth and pushing 10-year treasury yields upward over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 20-day yield changes in a majority of cases when both series rise month-over-month, or the interaction term in a regression is insignificant (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (defined by a rising federal funds rate over the past six months), strong nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reinforce the case for further rate hikes, increasing near-term yield expectations and driving the 10-year treasury yield higher over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is active, the 20-day yield change is negative in more than 60% of periods, or the predictive slope for PAYEMS changes sign.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP (GDPC1) signals strong aggregate demand, which leads the bond market to anticipate higher future interest rates and economic slack absorption, thereby pushing 10-year treasury yields higher over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of GDPC1 quarterly change vs 60-day forward yield change is below 0.05 over any 5-year period, or positive GDP growth leads to yield declines more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When retail and food services sales (RSAFS) exceed the threshold of 480,000 million dollars, it indicates robust consumer spending that boosts growth expectations and inflation pressures, causing the 10-year treasury yield to rise over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: After RSAFS crosses the threshold, 20-day forward yield changes are negative more often than positive, or the average yield change is below zero with a t-statistic > 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated above 245 and the federal funds rate remains low (below 1.5%), the combination erodes real interest rates and signals that the Fed is behind the curve, prompting bond investors to sell off long-term treasuries and driving yields higher over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: low rate environment with rising inflation
failure_condition: When the trigger is active and FEDFUNDS <= 1.5, the 20-day yield change is negative in a majority of cases, or the combined signal does not improve predictive performance over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a low fed funds rate environment (FEDFUNDS below 2%), a positive surprise in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 3-month average signals accelerating economic activity that pushes the short-term neutral rate higher, causing the 10-year treasury yield to rise over the next week as the market re-prices forward rates.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2% and INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average, the 5-day forward yield change is negative in more than 50% of cases or the mean change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).
